message:
style(tests): use std::slice::from_ref instead of vec-clone in proptests

Two tiny clippy cleanups in proptest bodies that were allocating a
single-element Vec just to pass a slice — `std::slice::from_ref`
takes the same reference without a heap allocation.

crates/fr-persist/src/lib.rs
  * `mr_aof_single_record_isolation`: `encode_aof_stream(&[record.clone()])`
    → `encode_aof_stream(std::slice::from_ref(&record))`. Avoids the
    `record.clone()` + single-element Vec inline construction that
    ran once per proptest iteration.
  * `mr_empty_aof_identity`: `fn(dummy in Just(())) { let _ = dummy; … }`
    → `fn(_dummy in Just(())) { … }`. proptest's `Just(())` strategy
    supplies a `()` value; the original body bound it and immediately
    discarded via `let _ = dummy;` — replace with a parameter-name
    prefix underscore so the "unused value" lint and the proptest
    argument-name convention agree.

crates/fr-store/tests/metamorphic.rs
  * `store.lpush(&key, &[v.clone()], 0)` →
    `store.lpush(&key, std::slice::from_ref(v), 0)` inside the
    `proptest! { }` block. Same rationale — one fewer allocation
    per case.
  * Identical replacement in three more proptests that were
    constructing a single-element slice for rpush / zadd / sadd.

No test logic change; the behaviors asserted on are identical.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
